summary: The test suite covers the main retention policy paths (max_age, keep_latest_n, max_total_size) and their combination through a fixture-based integration approach running via `act`. However, it has significant gaps: (1) no error scenario testing—invalid JSON, malformed dates, negative values, missing fields are untested; (2) no edge case coverage—single artifacts, same-timestamp sorting, all-deleted scenarios, policy conflicts not addressed; (3) brittle design relying on regex parsing of text output rather than structured assertions; (4) fixture files (.json) referenced but not provided, preventing full evaluation of test data quality; (5) no direct unit-level testing of individual functions, only integration testing through the full pipeline; (6) critical ambiguity unaddressed: task specifies 'keep-latest-N per workflow' but implementation groups by artifact name, and tests don't validate workflow_run_id semantics; (7) integration-only testing via `act` creates slow feedback loops and makes debugging difficult. While the test infrastructure (workflow validation, actionlint checks) is well-structured, the actual artifact-logic test coverage is shallow—primarily happy-path scenarios. Would catch major policy breaks but miss validation errors, edge cases, and error-path regressions.
